title: Cache Coherence: Write Invalidate and Write Update
summary: Defines the three conditions for cache coherence, describes snooping and directory protocols, and compares write-invalidate and write-update strategies.
content_points: Coherence conditions: (1) a processor reads what it last wrote; (2) another processor eventually sees the written value; (3) all processors agree on write order.
Write-through cache: coherence easier (main memory always current); write-back: harder.
Snooping: each cache monitors the bus for writes to lines it holds.
Directory: centralised record of which caches hold each line.
Write invalidate: invalidate all copies on write; lower bus traffic, preferred.
Write update: broadcast new value; lower latency for reads but higher bus traffic.
learning_objectives: State the three conditions for memory coherence
Compare snooping and directory coherence protocols
